seoTitle: Stoneville vs Stove Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stoneville and Stove Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stoneville vs Stove Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stoneville and Stove Hill.
heading: Stoneville vs Stove Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stoneville, Western Australia and Stove Hill, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stove Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Stoneville (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stoneville or Stove Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stoneville has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Stove Hill scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stoneville scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Stove Hill scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stoneville or Stove Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stove Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.67% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
